danger_signals.visual_form: Animated disaster sprites (tornado, flood, fire, earthquake, monster)
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent threat to city infrastructure and population; financial/population loss incoming
Power grid failure; zone will decline without electricity

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual change in zone appearance or crime overlay
danger_signals.meaning: Residential property values declining; industrial/commercial inefficiency

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual environmental degradation in zones near industrial areas
danger_signals.meaning: Health hazard; residential zone value decline; need for pollution controls

danger_signals.visual_form: Road network overlay showing congestion levels
danger_signals.meaning: Transport inadequacy; reduced commercial/industrial efficiency; population dissatisfaction

safety_signals.visual_form: Lit, developed residential tiles with visible housing structures
safety_signals.meaning: Stable population growth; citizens choosing to build and upgrade homes

safety_signals.visual_form: Developed commercial and industrial zone tiles with building sprites
safety_signals.meaning: Economic activity; tax revenue generation; balanced city sector development

safety_signals.visual_form: Road, rail, and transit infrastructure connecting zones
safety_signals.meaning: Adequate mobility; reduced crime potential; zone accessibility for workers

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear, undegraded visual appearance in residential areas
safety_signals.meaning: Health safety; property values stable or rising; population attracted to area

safety_signals.visual_form: Stable population growth visualization; no mass exodus of residents
safety_signals.meaning: Citizens satisfied with city services; sustainable revenue model

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Depopulated or demolished residential zones indicate abandonment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Residential zones cluster in low-pollution, well-powered, low-crime areas

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Abandoned or undeveloped commercial zones indicate poor zoning conditions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Commercial zones develop near residential areas and transportation networks

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Shuttered industrial zones indicate power loss or poor transport links
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Industrial zones create visible pollution; typically located away from residential areas due to pollution aversion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Power plants, water systems, transit infrastructure sprites
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Damaged or non-functional infrastructure appears disabled/darkened
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Power lines and transit routes visible as network overlay; critical to all sector development

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early city development (low population, limited budget)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sparse zone development; limited power grid; minimal road network; few buildings visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sims slowly construct basic residential housing; limited commercial/industrial growth until infrastructure expands

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Thriving city (high population, balanced sectors, good services)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dense developed zones; extensive power grid coverage; complex road/transit networks; diverse building types visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sims continuously upgrade housing; commercial/industrial zones actively develop; population growth visible

world_state_to_visual.world_state: City decline (high taxes, poor services, crime/pollution)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Zone degradation; darkened unpowered areas; visible abandonment; decreased building density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sims emigrate (population decline); zones devolve to lower density housing; commercial/industrial shutdown

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Animated disaster sprite (tornado funnel, flood water, fire, earthquake terrain shift, monster); affected zones destroyed or damaged
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Temporary population flight; infrastructure damage; revenue disruption; recovery period required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Infrastructure shutdown (power plants offline, roads unmaintained); widespread zone depopulation; visual deterioration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unable to build new structures; services collapse; population exodus accelerates; game-over condition approaches

environmental_storytelling.example: Industrial zones near residential areas visibly degrade property values; residential zones develop preferentially in low-pollution, well-serviced areas, naturally segregating sectors
Power grid expansion visibly lights up dark zones; transit networks enable zone clustering; player's infrastructure choices directly reflected in city layout and development patterns
Destroyed building sprites and cleared tiles show disaster impact; zones require rebuilding; visual scars persist until restoration occurs
Building density, zone upgrade levels, and population visible in zone appearance; thriving zones show dense development; struggling zones show sparse, degraded structures
Crime and pollution radiate visually from zones; player learns to isolate industrial areas and provide police/health services through visual cause-effect in zone development

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Construct and upgrade houses; populate zones fully; zone density increases visually
Abandon houses; emigrate; zone density visibly decreases; buildings devolve to lower-grade housing

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Thriving (good transport, residential proximity, adequate power)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Zone fills with commercial buildings; business structures develop and persist

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Failing (poor transport, isolated location, power loss)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Commercial buildings disappear; zone returns to undeveloped state

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Active (power available, transport access, not choked by pollution limits)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Industrial buildings persist; factories visible; pollution generated; tax revenue flows
Industrial buildings demolished; zone clears; pollution effects persist; revenue loss
Animated disaster sprite moves across map; destroys buildings and infrastructure in path; population disruption; financial impact immediate and visual

notes: SimCity communicates game state primarily through visible zone development density, building sprite diversity, and infrastructure visibility. The player learns to read city health through visual patterns: dense thriving zones vs. sparse abandoned zones, lit vs. dark areas, polluted vs. clean regions. The game uses implicit visual language—the player discovers adjacency rules and service requirements by observing which zones develop and which decline in response to zoning choices. Disaster visualization is explicit and immediate (animated sprites). Faction identity (residential/commercial/industrial) is implicit in building sprite differentiation and sector-specific behavior. Political/economic systems are communicated through gameplay feedback loops made visible: taxation level affects zone desirability (visible emigration); pollution affects residential values (zone decay); transport enables commerce (zone clustering). The Genesis port (1991) maintained this visual communication system from the original design.
